name: Django
what_it_does: High-level Python web framework that encourages rapid development with a batteries-included philosophy. Django provides ORM, authentication, admin interface, URL routing, templating, forms, migrations, and security middleware out of the box. Used for content management systems, e-commerce platforms, data dashboards, and REST APIs (via Django REST Framework). The most widely-deployed Python web framework.
use_cases: Build content-driven web applications with Django's built-in admin interface for CRUD management
Create REST APIs with Django REST Framework (DRF) for mobile app or SPA backends
Build applications needing built-in auth, permissions, and session management without integration work
Use Django's ORM with migrations for PostgreSQL, MySQL, or SQLite database management
Deploy data science or ML model serving APIs that need authentication and admin monitoring
not_for: High-performance async APIs — FastAPI with Uvicorn outperforms Django for async-first API workloads
Microservices requiring minimal footprint — Django's full-stack approach adds overhead for single-purpose services
Real-time applications requiring WebSockets — Django Channels adds complexity; consider FastAPI or dedicated WebSocket solutions
best_when: You're building feature-rich web applications that benefit from Django's built-in admin, ORM, auth, and the Django REST Framework ecosystem.
avoid_when: You need high-performance async API services — FastAPI is significantly faster and more ergonomic for async-first APIs.

auth.notes: Django is a framework with built-in auth. No external service auth required.

pricing.notes: Free and open source, maintained by the Django Software Foundation.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — self-hosted. WSGI sync model; async views available in Django 3.1+.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Django provides detailed error pages in DEBUG mode with variable inspection and stack traces. Production errors return 500 with logging. Form validation errors are field-level. ORM query errors include the SQL.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read views are idempotent. Write views require application-level idempotency. get_or_create() provides atomic upsert.
known_agent_gotchas: DEBUG=True must be False in production — debug mode exposes internal code, SQL queries, and settings to error pages; never deploy with DEBUG=True
Django ORM queries are lazy — QuerySets don't execute until evaluated (iteration, len(), bool()); chaining filters doesn't hit the database until consumption
N+1 query problem: accessing related objects in a loop triggers a query per object — always use select_related() for ForeignKey and prefetch_related() for ManyToMany
ALLOWED_HOSTS must be configured for production — leaving it empty causes 400 Bad Request for all requests; include the domain name and/or IP
Django migrations must be committed to version control — the migrations/ directory tracks schema history; missing or out-of-order migrations cause deployment failures
Django's CSRF protection applies to all POST/PUT/DELETE views by default — API views using DRF should use @api_view or DRF's authentication classes which handle CSRF appropriately

security_score_components.security_notes: Django includes CSRF, SQL injection, XSS, and clickjacking protection by default. SECRET_KEY must be kept secret and rotated. ALLOWED_HOSTS prevents host header injection. Django's security checklist is comprehensive.
